training_templates.title: Pharmaceutical Internship - English Speaking Training Template

template_sections.title: How To Use (For App Logic)
template_sections.notes: Each line can be played via speaker icon for pronunciation drills.

template_sections.title: Track 1 - Greeting
template_sections.notes: Speak slowly, friendly tone, smile when speaking.

template_sections.title: Track 2 - Program Introduction
template_sections.notes: Stress phrase: real-world experience. Pause naturally after each line.

template_sections.title: Track 4 - Instruction
template_sections.notes: Emphasize calibrate and record. Command tone but friendly.

template_lines.text_content: This training is designed to help instructors communicate effectively with American pharmacy students during a 6-week internship program.
Daily Communication
Lab (QC / R&D)
Production (GMP)
Warehouse
Presentation and Feedback
Roleplay and Real Scenarios
TEXT: for reading and TTS
NOTE: speaking tips
ROLEPLAY: interactive mode
PRACTICE: repetition
Recommended TTS speed: 0.85 to 0.95
Good morning everyone.
Welcome to our company.
My name is [Your Name], and I will be guiding you throughout this internship.
We are very happy to have you here.
Over the next six weeks, you will rotate through different departments including QC, R&D, production, and warehouse.
The goal is to give you real-world experience.
Could you briefly introduce yourself?
I am majoring in pharmacy.
That is great. What are you hoping to learn here?
This is the Quality Control lab.
Here, we test raw materials, in-process samples, and finished products.
Accuracy is very important in this lab.
Let us walk through the process together.
First, calibrate the instrument.
Then measure the sample.
Record the results immediately.
Why do we need to calibrate every time?
That is a great question. Calibration ensures accuracy. Even small deviations can affect the result.
This is the R&D lab.
We focus on developing new formulations, improving stability, and ensuring product effectiveness.
How do you choose excipients?
It depends on the formulation goals, such as stability, solubility, and release profile.
This is the production area.
Everything here follows GMP regulations.
Please follow all SOPs carefully.
Why is documentation so detailed?
Because traceability is critical. We need to track every step in case of issues.
This is our warehouse.
We store materials under controlled conditions.
Temperature and humidity are monitored continuously.
How do you manage inventory?
We use a digital system to track everything in real time.
You will present what you have learned.
Keep your presentation clear and structured: introduction, process, and conclusion.
That was a solid presentation.
One thing you could improve is explaining this part more clearly.
But overall, great job.
Let us walk through this.
Just make sure.
Go ahead and try.
Does that make sense?
That is a great question.
Keep going.
Explain -> Example -> Confirm
We test this parameter to ensure quality.
For example, if the pH is too high, stability may be affected.
Does that make sense?
Listen (TTS)
Repeat
Shadow
Record voice
Compare
I think you missed a step here.
Oh, I see.
No problem. Let us go through it again.
Why do we not automate this process?
That is a good point. In some cases we do, but manual control is still necessary.
